title: System, method and computer program product for computer assisted predictions generation
abstract: A computer-implemented method for computer assisted predictions generation, the method comprising executing on a processor the steps of: obtaining a predictions collection, the predictions collection comprising (a) a plurality of predictions generated by different people, (b) prediction parameters for each of the plurality of predictions, and (c) prediction outcome for each of the plurality of predictions, indicating a level of realization of the prediction; processing the predictions collection for detecting predictions parameters which result in high levels of realization of predictions; and modifying a computer assisted predictions generation process using a computerized environment for prediction generation.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A computer-implemented method for computer assisted predictions generation, the method comprising executing on a processor the steps of: obtaining a predictions collection, the predictions collection comprising (a) a plurality of predictions generated by different people, (b) prediction parameters for each of the plurality of predictions, and (c) prediction outcome for each of the plurality of predictions, indicating a le v el of realization of the prediction; processing the predictions collection for detecting predictions parameters which result in high levels of realization of predictions; and modifying a computer assisted predictions generation process using a computerized environment for prediction generation. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of predictions is a formalized user prediction which comprises a plurality of entities defined by an ontology. 3. The method according to claim 1, further comprising providing a computerized environment for generation of predictions by human members of at least one human analyst community including a multiplicity of human analyst end users. 4. The method according to claim 3, wherein the modifying comprises modifying a prediction generation parameters used by the computerized environment for generation of predictions based on user input. 5. The method according to claim 1, further comprising providing a computerized environment for review of predictions by human members of at least one human analyst community including a multiplicity of human analyst end users. 6. The method according to claim 5, wherein the modifying includes modifying a prediction review parameter used by the computerized environment for review of predictions based on user input. 7. A non-transitory computer-readable medium for computer assisted predictions generation, comprising instructions stored thereon, that when executed on a processor, perform the steps of: obtaining a predictions collection, the predictions collection comprising (a) a plurality of predictions generated by different people, (b) prediction parameters for each of the plurality of predictions, and (c) prediction outcome for each of the plurality of predictions, indicating a le vel of realization of the prediction; processing the predictions collection for detecting predictions parameters which result in high levels of realization of predictions; and modifying a computer assisted predictions generation process using a computerized environment for prediction generation. 8. The non -transitory computer-readable medium according to claim 7, wherein each of the plurality of predictions is a formalized user prediction which comprises a plurality of entities defined by an ontology. 9. The non -transitor ' computer-readable medium according to claim 7, further comprising instructions for providing a computerized environment for generation of predictions by human members of at least one human analyst community including a multiplicity of human analyst end users. 10. The non-transitory computer-readable medium according to claim 9, wherein the instructions for modifying comprises instructions for modifying a prediction generation parameters used by the computerized environment for generation of predictions based on user input. 1 1. The non-transitory computer-readable medium according to claim 7, further comprising instructions for providing a computerized environment for review of predictions by human members of at least one human analyst community including a multiplicity of human analyst end users. 12. The non-transitory computer-readable medium according to claim 11, wherein the instructions for modifying comprises instructions for modifying a prediction 

 review parameter used by the computerized environment for review of predictions based on user input. 13. A computer assisted predictions generation system, the system comprising: a tangible memory module, operable to store a predictions collection, the predictions collection comprising (a) a plurality of predictions generated by different people, (b) prediction parameters for each of the plurality of predictions, and (c) prediction outcome for each of the plurality of predictions, indicating a level of realization of the prediction; a processor, operable to: process the predictions collection for detecting predictions parameters which result in high levels of realization of predictions; and to modify a computer assisted predictions generation process using a computerized environment for prediction generation. 14. The system according to claim 13, wherein each of the plurality of predictions is a foirnalized user prediction which comprises a plurality of entities defined by an ontology. 15. The system according to claim 13, further comprising at least one hardware user interface for receiving user input, and a computerized environment for generation of predictions by human members of at least one human analyst community including a multiplicity of human analyst end users. 16. The system according to claim. 15, wherein the processor is operable to modify the computer assisted predictions generation process at least by modifying a prediction generation parameters used by the computerized environment for generation of predictions based on user input. 17. The system according to claim 13, further comprising at least one hardware user interface for receiving user input, and a computerized environment for review of 

 predictions by human members of at least one human analyst community including a multiplicity of human analyst end users. 18. The system according to claim 17, wherein the processor is operable to modify the computer assisted predictions generation process at least by modifying a prediction review parameter used by the computerized environment for review of predictions based on user input.
